text: What if I told you your kidneys play a secret role in your blood pressure? Meet the renin-angiotensin system, a critical player in regulating blood flow and pressure. When blood pressure drops, kidneys release renin, triggering a cascade of reactions. This process produces angiotensin, which narrows blood vessels and raises pressure. Understanding this system may help manage conditions like hypertension, but it’s complex. Curious about how your body keeps balance? Follow for more science insights!
